name: Peanut Butter "King Cake" Cookie
recipeIngredient: 8 Chocolate kisses or Chocolate candy-coated pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line three baking sheets with foil. Grease with shortening.
2. For cookies, combine shortening, brown sugar, peanut butter, milk and vanilla in a large bowl. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended. Add egg. Beat just until blended.
3. Combine flour, salt and baking soda. Add to creamed mixture on low speed. Mix just until blended.
4. Divide dough into 3 equal parts. Working with 1/3 of the dough at a time, break off 8 equal pieces of dough. Roll into balls. Hide one Chocolate kiss in one of the 8 balls, molding dough with fingers around chocolate kiss (this is the prize). Arrange balls on baking sheet almost touching in a 6-inch (15 cm) circle. Flatten balls slightly with fingers. Repeat with remaining balls to make two more circles.
5. Bake one baking sheet at a time at 375°F (190°C) for 9 to 11 minutes, or until set. Do not overbake. Cool 5 to 8 minutes on baking sheet before removing cookie on foil to countertop to cool completely.
6. For icing, combine icing sugar, shortening, milk and corn syrup in a bowl. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until smooth. If too thick, add additional milk, a few drops at a time. If too thin, add additional icing sugar.
7. Divide icing into half. Place 1/2 into a large bowl. Divide the other half into three small bowls. Add yellow food coloring to one small bowl until desired shade is achieved and mix well. Add equal amounts of yellow and blue food coloring to the second small bowl to make green icing. Add equal amounts of blue and red food coloring to the third small bowl to make purple icing. Place colored icings in small resealable plastic bags. Seal. Cut a tiny tip off the corner of each bag.
8. Frost each cookie with white icing using a spatula or knife. Pipe colored icings decoratively over each "King cake." Makes: 3 King cake cookies
